Job.JobTitle: Instructional Consultant for General Education Students K-12
Job.JobDescription:
Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
Instructional Consultant for General Education Students K-12, SES ABOUT US AND OUR CULTURE At the Genesee Intermediate School District, we are collaborators, leaders, educators, and support staff delivering instructional programs and services in general education, special education, and career technical education to 64,000 students, their families, and over 8,500 educators. GISD values its staff and cultivates positivity in the workplace as we all work together to carry out our mission of leadership, service, and innovation – partnering for success! GISD promotes a collaborative work environment with a strong emphasis on teamwork and professional growth. We are committed to fostering a positive and supportive culture where all staff members feel valued and respected. Our dedication to professional development is demonstrated through initiatives such as resources for professional learning, mentorship programs, and leadership skill development. We are devoted to creating a thriving organizational culture that benefits not only our staff, but also the students and families we serve. To learn more about the GISD culture, please click the link below: GISD Standards, Personalized Service and Quality Programs SALARY AND BENEFITS Salary: $51,373 - $90,934, commensurate with experience and qualifications Benefits: Our comprehensive benefits package includes health insurance with five different plans, dental and vision coverage, life and disability insurance, voluntary insurance options, and a supplemental life program. Employees are entitled to sick time, vacation time, bereavement days, holidays, and longevity pay. Flexible spending accounts (FSA) or healthcare savings accounts (HSA) are available based on the elected medical plan. Additionally, retirement savings plans are provided through the State of Michigan Office of Retirement Services (ORS). These benefits are available after just one full day of employment. LOCATION 5075 Pilgrim Rd. Flint, MI 48507 ROLE AND ESSENTIAL DUTIES The Instructional Consultant provides direct intervention services to students and also serves in a consultative/supportive role with staff, students, parents, and representatives from local districts and community agencies. Responsible for coordination, development, and implementation of instructional practices within assigned areas within a Multi-Tiered System of Supports (MTSS) framework. Essential Duties (May include, but not limited to): Provides direct intervention services for students identified as needing additional tiered support. Provides consultation to classroom staff in areas such as instructional methods, evaluation, and problem solving. Communicates and works cooperatively with school district personnel to promote and facilitate the development and delivery of instruction and behavioral support within a multi-tiered system of support. Participates in district MTSS meetings when appropriate. Assists teachers with designing instruction based on assessment data. Assists in the development, maintenance, and documentation of student’s Plan of Care and related service provision. Prepares reports to facilitate and document student services. Provides teachers resources and training related to instruction and curriculum. Demonstrates knowledge of current research-based methodologies and instructional strategies that engage students. Provides instructional strategies and evidence-based practices using data to monitor progress. Provides coaching and modeling of evidence-based practices to staff. Demonstrates knowledge of using a variety of assessment tools. Provides support in analyzing student assessment data. Consistently models Win-Win leadership. Other Duties: May serve as a resource for the development of department budget and district plan goals related to the assigned area. May serve as a resource person to local school district school improvement teams related to the assigned area of responsibility. Represents the district on various community agencies and professional organizations as appropriate to the assigned area. Performs other related duties as assigned. QUALIFICATIONS Education: Master's degree required; valid Michigan teacher certificate required. Experience: Minimum of three years of successful teaching experience. Skills/Other: Ability to assist staff with the implementation of their job responsibilities and demonstrated skills in team planning. Ability to consult with administration regarding the individualized needs of students. Excellent written and oral communication skills. Excellent interpersonal skills. Ability to work a flexible workday, work week, and work year. Ability to travel to and from the intermediate school district to constituent districts as needed. Ability to utilize district technology and work to maintain proficiency, as required skills sets change with technology and/or the needs of the district. Demonstrated physical stamina. Demonstrate honesty, integrity, and professionalism at all times. Exhibit emotional stability, exercise good judgment, and make decisions in accordance with board policies and administrative guidelines. DEADLINE AND APPLICATION PROCESS Current GISD employees who wish to apply for the vacancy should apply online using the job postings link accessible from the home page of the GISD website. Under the job postings category, select the link titled Current Genesee ISD Employees - click here for vacancies, located under the "Internal Staff" heading. External candidates may apply by selecting the Apply link above and completing the online application and uploading a resume. If you do not have access to a scanner, plan to bring this documentation, if you are selected to interview. Transcripts printed from the Internet are not acceptable. Please do not send paper documentation to Human Resources. Join us in shaping the future of education. Interested candidates should submit their resume, cover letter, and any other relevant documents to the GISD Human Resources department. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled. Join a forward-thinking team that makes a lasting impact on students, families and the community. Apply today to become a part of the Genesee Intermediate School District team! An Equal Opportunity Employer
Job.CompanyDescription: Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is looking for a Track & Field coach to join our program. Apply now.
Hamady High School — Athletic Secretary Department: Athletics Reports to: Athletic Director Work Year: 10-Month Position Bargaining Unit: WHESPA (Westwood Heights Educational Support Personnel Association) Status: Non-exempt, Classified/Secretarial Position Overview The Athletic Secretary provides comprehensive administrative and logistical support to the Hamady High School Athletic Department, ensuring efficient daily operations, accurate record keeping, regulatory compliance, and timely communication among coaches, student-athletes, families, officials, and visiting schools. Duties & Responsibilities Office & Communication Serve as the primary front-office contact for athletics—greet visitors, answer calls, and respond to inquiries professionally. Prepare and distribute correspondence, memos, schedules, announcements, and meeting materials. Maintain agendas and minutes for athletic meetings and communication with internal and external stakeholders. Scheduling & Event Logistics Maintain the master athletics calendar and publish weekly schedules. Communicate changes, postponements, and cancellations to administration, coaches, families, officials, custodians, transportation, and media partners. Arrange game officials, event workers (ticket takers, clock operators, scorekeepers), and supervision for home events. Coordinate set-up for home contests, banquets, senior recognition ceremonies, award events, and college signing days. Prepare, distribute, and track Seasonal Coach Contracts , ensuring proper documentation and compliance. Transportation & Facilities Coordinate transportation for all athletic events and field trips; manage schedule changes and maintain cost tracking. Oversee and schedule facility use for gyms, fields, and after-school athletics; communicate updates to custodial and maintenance teams. Compliance & Student-Athlete Records Maintain confidential student-athlete files, including physicals, consent forms, emergency contact information, and medical records. Track academic and athletic eligibility under MHSAA and district guidelines. Support coaches in meeting MHSAA and district requirements for participation and compliance. Financial & Ticketing Responsibilities Process purchase orders, requisitions, invoices, deposits, and athletic payments. Reconcile gate receipts and concession revenue; maintain accurate financial logs. Organize ticketing for athletic events and ensure proper event-day and post-event financial procedures. Communication, Media & Display Responsibilities Maintain inventories for equipment, supplies, awards, and office materials. Assist with athletic website updates, community notifications, and announcements as directed. Provide Weekly Updates to the Athletic Image Board, including announcements, highlights, upcoming events, and seasonal recognition items. Additional Responsibilities Support coordination of district-approved communication platforms. Assist coaches with roster creation and essential materials for parent meetings. Contribute to social media updates, sports program materials, and event publications. Perform other duties as assigned in support of the athletic program. Required Qualifications High school diploma or equivalent. Minimum of two (2) years office/secretarial experience, preferably in a school setting. Proficiency in Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and athletic scheduling platforms. Strong organizational skills and ability to handle multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Ability to maintain confidentiality and work professionally with students, staff, parents, and the public. Preferred Qualifications Knowledge of MHSAA rules and athletic eligibility requirements. Experience in bookkeeping or financial record management. Current First Aid/CPR/AED certification or willingness to obtain. Work Conditions High-traffic office environment with frequent interruptions and strict deadline requirements. Some evening and weekend hours required for athletic events and seasonal demands. Ability to stand, sit, bend, and lift up to 25–40 lbs as needed. Salary & Benefits (Per WHESPA Bargaining Agreement) Work Year: This is a 10-month position, aligned with the district academic calendar. Additional hours may be required during peak athletic seasons. Salary and benefits per the WHESPA contract terms. Application Process Complete online application and submit the following: Resume Cover letter Three professional references Relevant certifications (if applicable)

Job.JobTitle: Speech and Language Pathologist - Early On
Job.JobDescription:
Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
Speech and Language Pathologist-Early On ABOUT US AND OUR CULTURE At the Genesee Intermediate School District, we are collaborators, leaders, educators, and support staff delivering instructional programs and services in general education, special education, and career technical education to 64,000 students, their families, and over 8,500 educators. GISD values its staff and cultivates positivity in the workplace as we all work together to carry out our mission of leadership, service, and innovation – partnering for success! GISD promotes a collaborative work environment with a strong emphasis on teamwork and professional growth. We are committed to fostering a positive and supportive culture where all staff members feel valued and respected. Our dedication to professional development is demonstrated through initiatives such as resources for professional learning, mentorship programs, and leadership skill development. We are devoted to creating a thriving organizational culture that benefits not only our staff, but also the students and families we serve. To learn more about the GISD culture, please click the link below: GISD Standards, Personalized Service and Quality Programs SALARY AND BENEFITS Salary: $52,361 - $92,683 annually, commensurate with experience and qualifications Benefits: Our comprehensive benefits package includes health insurance with five different plans, dental and vision coverage, life and disability insurance, voluntary insurance options, and a supplemental life program. Employees are entitled to sick time, vacation time, bereavement days, holidays, and longevity pay. Flexible spending accounts (FSA) or healthcare savings accounts (HSA) are available based on the elected medical plan. Additionally, retirement savings plans are provided through the State of Michigan Office of Retirement Services (ORS). These benefits are available after just one full day of employment. LOCATION 5075 Pilgrim Road Flint, MI 48507 ROLE AND ESSENTIAL DUTIES The Speech and Language Pathologist for Early On serves as part of a team that works to meet the developmental needs of children, through a multi-tiered system of support. The Speech/ and Language Pathologist uses a coaching model to share, with parents/caregivers/educational team members, strategies to help the child develop new skills; services are provided in the child’s natural environment. Essential Duties (May include, but not limited to): Assists in developing a multi-tiered system of support to provide developmental and educational experiences for children that impact their development. Develops, implements, and evaluates individualized educational plans for children birth to age three. Provides direct services indicated in the child’s individual family service plan. Provides consultation to families and team members to assist in addressing developmental concerns. Identifies the need for and provide cross training for team members. Follows state and federal rules when developing a child’s individual family service plan. Participates in child’s assessments. Interprets evaluation results for families. Utilizes technology to collect and maintain detailed data on child’s progress. Provides appropriate activities and strategies for children based on how they learn and interact with their natural environment. Demonstrates knowledge of evidenced-based and developmentally-appropriate practices. Demonstrates an understanding of development of language/communication skills in young children and coaching caregivers in strategies and activities to improve language/communication skills. Consistently models Win-Win leadership. Other Duties: Drives to and from service sites, including homes and/or childcare settings to deliver services and coaching strategies within a Primary Service Provider Model. Represents GISD on various committee groups in the community including presentations to community agencies about Early Intervention Services in Genesee County. Performs other related duties as assigned. QUALIFICATIONS Education: Master's degree in speech and language pathology. Certificate of Clinical Competence (CCC) from the American Speech-Language-Hearing Association (ASHA). Experience: Evidence of successful work with children with disabilities, birth-to-three years of age preferred. Skills/Other: Ability to work effectively with children and adults individually and in a group setting. Ability to communicate verbally and in written communications. Ability to utilize district technology and work to maintain proficiency, as required skill sets change with technology and/or the needs of the district. Ability to drive to and from homes and community centers. Ability to participate in professional development related to essential duties. Demonstrated physical and mental ability and stamina for the job. Punctuality and good attendance are requirements for the position. Ability to work a flexible workday, work week, and work year. Represents the district in a professional manner at all times. Demonstrate honesty, integrity, and professionalism at all times. Ability to exercise good judgment and make decisions in accordance with board policies and established administrative guidelines with minimum supervision. DEADLINE AND APPLICATION PROCESS Current GISD employees who wish to apply for the vacancy should apply online using the job postings link accessible from the home page of the GISD website. Under the job postings category, select the link titled Current Genesee ISD Employees - click here for vacancies, located under the "Internal Staff" heading. External candidates may apply by selecting the Apply link above and completing the online application and uploading a resume. If you do not have access to a scanner, plan to bring this documentation, if you are selected to interview. Transcripts printed from the Internet are not acceptable. Please do not send paper documentation to Human Resources. Join us in shaping the future of education. Interested candidates should submit their resume, cover letter, and any other relevant documents to the GISD Human Resources department. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled. Join a forward-thinking team that makes a lasting impact on students, families and the community. Apply today to become a part of the Genesee Intermediate School District team! An Equal Opportunity Employer
Job.Experience: Evidence of successful work with children with disabilities, birth-to-three years of age preferred.
Job.Benefits: Our comprehensive benefits package includes health insurance with five different plans, dental and vision coverage, life and disability insurance, voluntary insurance options, and a supplemental life program. Employees are entitled to sick time, vacation time, bereavement days, holidays, and longevity pay. Flexible spending accounts (FSA) or healthcare savings accounts (HSA) are available based on the elected medical plan. Additionally, retirement savings plans are provided through the State of Michigan Office of Retirement Services (ORS). These benefits are available after just one full day of employment.

Job.JobTitle: Speech and Language Pathologist - Up to $12k 2026/27 Sign On Bonus Available!
Job.JobDescription:
Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Speech-Language Pathologist plays a crucial role in assessing, diagnosing, and treating communication disorders in students to support their academic and social success. They provide individualized and group therapy, develop intervention plans, and collaborate with teachers, parents, and specialists to integrate language development strategies into the classroom. Additionally, they participate in student evaluations, Individualized Education Program (IEP) meetings, and maintain accurate documentation to ensure compliance with special education requirements. By working closely with families and community resources, they help create a supportive learning environment that fosters student growth and communication skills. Please see attached job description for a detailed list of the essential functions. Salary & Benefits: Teacher Salary Schedule + Up to $12,000 commitment bonus* 183 days per year Health, dental, vision, life and disability insurance. Employees shall be subject to the co-payment of health care costs equal to the difference between the premium cost and the annual cost limitation imposed on public employers. *The commitment bonus shall be distributed to eligible teachers based on available and sustainable funds and payable in accordnce with District policy and guidelines. Education: Master's degree required in speech-language pathology or a valid Michigan teacher's certificate with full approval in the speech and language impaired required. A valid Speech-Language Pathologist license from the State of Michigan. Certificate of Clinical Competence in Speech-Language Pathology (CCC-SLP) from the American Speech-Language-Hearing Association (ASHA) is required. Clinical fellows with Educational Limited Speech-Language Pathologist license from the State of Michigan will be considered, on-site clinical fellowship supervision is available. Experience: Previous experience with coaching others on strategies to improve language and communications skills in students preferred. An online application is required for all positions. In addition, all applicants must submit the following: Signed cover letter Current resume Copy of transcripts Three signed professional letters of recommendation (written within the last 18 months preferred) These documents must be scanned and uploaded through the online application process. ** Early application is encouraged as positions may close when filled. **

Job.JobTitle: Teacher Consultant for Special Education Compliance and Technical Assistance - Special Education Services
Job.JobDescription:
Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
Teacher Consultant for Special Education Compliance and Technical Assistance - Special Education Services ABOUT US AND OUR CULTURE At the Genesee Intermediate School District, we are collaborators, leaders, educators, and support staff delivering instructional programs and services in general education, special education, and career technical education to 64,000 students, their families, and over 8,500 educators. GISD values its staff and cultivates positivity in the workplace as we all work together to carry out our mission of leadership, service, and innovation  partnering for success! GISD promotes a collaborative work environment with a strong emphasis on teamwork and professional growth. We are committed to fostering a positive and supportive culture where all staff members feel valued and respected. Our dedication to professional development is demonstrated through initiatives such as resources for professional learning, mentorship programs, and leadership skill development. We are devoted to creating a thriving organizational culture that benefits not only our staff, but also the students and families we serve. To learn more about the GISD culture, please click the link below: GISD Standards, Personalized Service and Quality Programs SALARY AND BENEFITS Salary: $52,361 - $92,683 annually, commensurate with experience and qualifications Benefits: Our comprehensive benefits package includes health insurance with five different plans, dental and vision coverage, life and disability insurance, voluntary insurance options, and a supplemental life program. Employees are entitled to sick time, vacation time, bereavement days, holidays, and longevity pay. Flexible spending accounts (FSA) or healthcare savings accounts (HSA) are available based on the elected medical plan. Additionally, retirement savings plans are provided through the State of Michigan Office of Retirement Services (ORS). These benefits are available after just one full day of employment. LOCATION 5075 Pilgrim Road Flint, MI 48507 ROLE AND ESSENTIAL DUTIES The Consultant for Special Education Compliance and Technical Assistance serves in a consultive/supportive role with staff, students, parents, and representatives from local districts and community agencies. Responsible for coordination, development, and coaching within assigned areas. Essential Duties (May include, but not limited to): Participates as part of the General Supervision and Monitoring (GSM) team to support special education compliance and the delivery of services and specially designed instruction to students in the least restrictive environment. Provides consultation to districts demonstrating noncompliance regarding policies, practices, and procedures for special education. Provides professional development activities to school district personnel. Serves on the GISD MTSS team and supports district teams in system development to implement Multi-Tiered System of Supports (MTSS) Guides LEA problem solving and implementation planning through a data based decision making process designed to use capacity, fidelity, and student outcome data. Prepares reports to facilitate, document, or evaluate programs and assigned duties. Consults, counsels, and collaborates with parents, school personnel, students, and appropriate community agencies regarding educational concerns. Assist teachers in planning, sequencing, and scaffolding of instruction. Models techniques for effective delivery of instruction in classrooms. Assists in staff development activities related to assigned duties. Remains up to date on current methodologies and strategies for instructional programs for students with disabilities. Consistently models Win-Win leadership. Other Duties: May serve as a resource for the development of department budget and district plan goals related to the assigned area. May serve as a resource person to local school district school improvement teams related to the assigned area of responsibility. Represents the district on various community agencies and professional organizations as appropriate to the assigned area. Performs other related duties in the department as assigned. QUALIFICATIONS Education: Master's degree required; valid Michigan teacher certificate with an endorsement in one or more areas of special education, licensed special education teacher meeting requirements for teacher consultant approval is required. Experience: Minimum of three years of satisfactory experience working with students with disabilities in a school setting is required. Skills/Other: Ability to communicate clearly and concisely in written and oral communication with administrators, staff, parents/guardians, and community agencies. Demonstrated enthusiasm and a positive attitude. Ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships with students, peers, parents, school personnel, and community agencies. Ability to read, analyze, and interpret general business periodicals, professional journals, technical procedures, and government regulations. Knowledgeable about educational organizations, educational law, and the areas of educational assessment and evaluation. Ability to utilize district technology and work to maintain proficiency, as required skill sets change with technology and the needs of the district. Experience with collecting literacy data. Demonstrated mental/physical ability and stamina for meeting the requirements of the position. Works successfully as a member of the team. Has the ability to utilize meeting outcomes to effectively run meetings, action plan, and follow through utilizing a coaching model. Demonstrate honesty, integrity, and professionalism at all times. Ability to travel to and from GISD, constituent school districts, students' homes and other agencies. Ability to work a flexible schedule. Punctuality and good attendance are requirements for the position. Exercise good judgment and make decisions in accordance with board policies and established administrative guidelines. DEADLINE AND APPLICATION PROCESS Current GISD employees who wish to apply for the vacancy should apply online using the job postings link accessible from the home page of the GISD website. Under the job postings category, select the link titled Current Genesee ISD Employees - click here for vacancies, located under the "Internal Staff" heading. External candidates may apply by selecting the Apply link above and completing the online application and uploading a resume. If you do not have access to a scanner, plan to bring this documentation, if you are selected to interview. Transcripts printed from the Internet are not acceptable. Please do not send paper documentation to Human Resources. If you have any questions or need assistance please email our Human Resources Department at GISDHR@geneseeisd.org or call 810.591.4260 Join us in shaping the future of education. Interested candidates should submit their resume, cover letter, and any other relevant documents to the GISD Human Resources department. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled. Join a forward-thinking team that makes a lasting impact on students, families and the community. Apply today to become a part of the Genesee Intermediate School District team! An Equal Opportunity Employer

Job.JobTitle: Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Assistant Basketball Coach/Girls Sub-Varsity
Job.JobDescription:
Huron Valley Schools is looking for a Basketball Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Job Title: Girls Sub-Varsity/Assistant Basketball Coach Location: Southfield A&T High School Reports To: Athletic Director Start Date/End Dates: November 2025 – March 2026 Salary: $2,700.00 Stipend per the SEA CBA (paid at the end of the season) Job Overview: The Girls’ Basketball Coach is responsible for overseeing and managing all aspects of the girls’ basketball program, including coaching, player development, team coordination, and communication with players, parents, and staff. The ideal candidate will be dedicated to building a competitive and supportive team environment, fostering the personal and athletic growth of student-athletes, and promoting the values of teamwork, sportsmanship, and discipline. Responsibilities: Coaching: Lead and assist the Head Coach in the girls’ basketball team practices and during games, providing skill development, strategy, and motivation to players. Player Development: Foster the growth of individual players in both athletic and personal aspects, promoting physical conditioning, mental toughness, and sportsmanship. Team Management: Develop and implement training sessions, drills, and practice plans that improve player performance and team cohesion. Game Day Management: Prepare the team for competitions, make real-time decisions during games, and conduct post-game analysis and feedback sessions. Scheduling & Coordination: Organize and manage the team’s practice schedule, game schedule, travel arrangements, and other logistical details. Communication: Maintain open communication with players, parents, athletic department staff, and other school officials to provide updates and foster team morale. Compliance & Safety: Ensure that the team follows all safety protocols, academic eligibility requirements, and sportsmanship expectations set by the school, district, and governing athletic organizations. Growing the Program: Work closely with the Athletic Director and the Middle Schools for retention and preparation for the next season. Fundraising & Team Support: Assist in fundraising efforts for the program, including organizing events, seeking sponsorships, and managing team budgets. Mentorship: Serve as a role model and mentor for student-athletes, encouraging academic achievement, discipline, and respect both on and off the court. Qualifications: Previous experience coaching basketball at the high school or college level preferred. Strong understanding of basketball techniques, strategies, and game rules. Excellent leadership, communication, and organizational skills. Ability to motivate and inspire student-athletes to reach their full potential. Strong commitment to the development of student-athletes both on and off the court. Ability to work effectively with a diverse group of students, parents, and staff. Must pass a background check and meet all district and state coaching requirements. Physical Requirements: Ability to participate in and demonstrate basketball techniques during practice and games. Ability to lift and carry coaching equipment as needed.

Job.JobTitle: Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Competitive Cheer Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Huron Valley Schools is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Job Title: Competitive Cheer Coach Reports To: Building Principal/Athletic Director Location: Southfield A&T Start Date/End Date: November 2025-March 2026 Salary: $1,900.00 Stipend per the SEA CBA (paid at the end of the season based on the payment calendar) Job Summary: The Cheer Coach is responsible for organizing, planning, and leading the cheerleading program. This includes teaching cheers, stunts, and dance routines; promoting school spirit; always ensuring safety; and fostering teamwork, discipline, and leadership among team members. The coach will work closely with the athletic department, school administration, and parents to support athletic events, competitions, and school functions. Key Responsibilities: Plan and conduct practices focused on cheer techniques, stunting, tumbling, and performance. Lead the cheer team in promoting school spirit at games, pep rallies, and special events. Choreograph routines for halftime shows, assemblies, and competitions. Always emphasize safety, particularly during stunts and tumbling activities. Promote positive team culture, including sportsmanship, responsibility, and leadership. Ensure all participants meet eligibility, academic, and attendance requirements. Manage uniforms, equipment, and team fundraising efforts. Maintain open and professional communication with student-athletes, parents, and school staff. Supervise athletes during all practices, games, competitions, and travel (if applicable). Support and enforce all school, district, and league policies. Qualifications: Prior experience as a cheer coach at the high school or club level or higher required. Knowledge of cheer techniques, stunts, and performance skills. Ability to demonstrate skills and drills and routines. Strong leadership, organization, and communication skills. Must be able to supervise and motivate students in a positive manner. Experience with music editing and choreography.

Job.JobTitle: Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Baseball Head Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Huron Valley Schools is looking for a Baseball Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Job Title: Baseball Head Coach Reports To: Athletic Director Location: Southfield A&T High School Start Date/End Date: November 2025- March 2026 Salary: $4,200 Stipend per SEA/CBA paid at the end of the season based on the payment calendar. Job Summary: The Head Baseball Coach is responsible for developing, leading, and maintaining a competitive high school baseball program that aligns with the mission and goals of the school’s athletic department. The coach will provide leadership that promotes sportsmanship, teamwork, skill development, and academic success among student-athletes. Key Responsibilities: Organize, plan, and oversee all aspects of the baseball program, including practices, games, and offseason conditioning. Teach proper baseball fundamentals, mechanics, and strategies appropriate to high school competition. Develop detailed practice plans that focus on skill progression, team cohesion, and player safety. Foster a culture of academic accountability, teamwork, sportsmanship, and respect. Monitor and support the academic progress of student-athletes to ensure eligibility and success in the classroom. Supervise all team activities, including travel to away games and tournaments. Manage and maintain baseball equipment, uniforms, and facilities. Communicate effectively with players, parents, and school officials regarding schedules, expectations, and program needs. Ensure adherence to district, league, and state association rules and regulations. Work collaboratively with assistant coaches to ensure consistent instruction and program development. Serve as a positive representative of the baseball program within the school and local community. Qualifications: Previous baseball coaching experience at the high school/club level or higher required. Comprehensive knowledge of baseball fundamentals, techniques, and strategies. CPR, First Aid, and Concussion Awareness certification (or willingness to obtain before the season). Strong organizational, communication, and leadership skills. Demonstrated ability to motivate and perform skills and drills. Must meet all district and state athletic association certification and training requirement

Job.JobDescription:
Huron Valley Schools is looking for a Baseball Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.

Job.JobTitle: Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Soccer Head Coach (GIRLS)
Job.JobDescription:
Huron Valley Schools is looking for a Soccer Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Job Title: Soccer Head Coach (GIRLS) Reports To: Athletic Director Location: Southfield A&T High School Start Date/End Date: March2026- May 2026 Salary: $4,200 Stipend per SEA/CBA paid at the end of the season based on the payment calendar. Job Summary: The Soccer Coach is responsible for leading, developing, and managing the soccer program while fostering a positive and competitive environment. This includes planning practices, coaching games, mentoring student-athletes, and promoting sportsmanship, discipline, and teamwork. The coach will work collaboratively with school staff, parents, and athletic department leadership to ensure the program's success on and off the field. Key Responsibilities: Plan, organize, and run effective soccer practices and training sessions. Teach fundamental and advanced soccer techniques, strategies, and game rules. Prepare the team for competitions and manage all aspects of game-day coaching. Promote academic success, character development, and personal growth among players. Ensure the safety and well-being of all athletes during practices, games, and travel. Maintain equipment inventory and ensure proper use and storage. Coordinate schedules with the athletic department and manage logistics for practices and games. Communicate regularly with athletes, parents, and school administration. Promote a culture of sportsmanship, discipline, accountability, and respect. Attend league meetings, professional development opportunities, and obtain required certifications. Follow and enforce all school, district, and athletic department policies and procedures. Qualifications: Prior coaching experience in soccer at the high school level, club level or higher. Complete understanding of the game, including rules, strategies, and player development. Ability to demonstrate skills and drills. Strong leadership, communication, and organizational skills. Ability to work with diverse groups of student-athletes and staff. CPR/First Aid certification (or willingness to obtain).

Job.JobTitle: Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Softball Head Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Huron Valley Schools is looking for a Softball Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Job Title: Softball Head Coach Reports To: Athletic Director Location: Southfield A&T High School Start Date/End Date: November 2025- March 2026 Salary: $4,200 Stipend per SEA/CBA paid at the end of the season based on the payment calendar. Job Summary: The Head Softball Coach is responsible for leading, organizing, and developing all aspects of the high school softball program in alignment with the school’s athletic philosophy and district goals. The coach will foster a positive and competitive environment that emphasizes player development, teamwork, accountability, and sportsmanship. Key Responsibilities: Organize and supervise all levels of the softball program, including tryouts, practices, games, and offseason conditioning. Teach and develop fundamental and advanced softball skills such as batting mechanics, pitching techniques, defensive positioning, and base running. Develop and implement structured practice plans designed to improve team performance and player confidence. Foster teamwork, sportsmanship, and a culture of respect, discipline, and hard work. Communicate clearly and consistently with student-athletes, parents, and school personnel regarding schedules, expectations, and team progress. Monitor academic progress and eligibility of all players; encourage balance between athletics and academics. Ensure compliance with district, league, and state athletic association regulations. Maintain inventory of uniforms, equipment, and supplies and ensure safety standards are met. Supervise athletes during all practices, games, travel, and team events Qualifications: Previous coaching and/or competitive softball experience required at the high school level/club or higher required. Strong knowledge of softball rules, techniques, and game strategy. Valid CPR, First Aid, and Concussion Awareness certification (or ability to obtain prior to season). Excellent communication, leadership, and organizational skills. Demonstrated ability to motivate and teach athletes skills and drills. Must meet all district, league, and state athletic association certification requirements.

Job.JobTitle: Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Swim Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Huron Valley Schools is looking for a Swimming and Diving Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Job Title: Swim Coach Reports To: Athletic Director Location: Southfield A&T High School Start Date/End Date: November 2025- March 2026 Salary: $4,200 Stipend per SEA/CBA paid at the end of the season based on the payment calendar. Job Summary: The Swimming Coach is responsible for organizing, directing, and supervising the school’s swimming program in accordance with district policies and state athletic association guidelines. The coach promotes student-athlete growth and development, fosters a positive team culture, and ensures safety, sportsmanship, and academic balance for all participants. Key Responsibilities: Plan, organize, and supervise all phases of the swimming program, including tryouts, practices, and competitions. Teach proper swimming techniques, conditioning, and stroke development while emphasizing safety and injury prevention. Develop daily, weekly, and seasonal practice plans that promote athlete improvement and team success. Communicate effectively with athletes, parents, and school administration regarding schedules, expectations, and program needs. Model and promote sportsmanship, teamwork, and respect at all times. Ensure all athletes meet eligibility requirements as set by the school, district, and state association. Maintain accurate attendance records, equipment inventory, and meet results. Coordinate the use of pool facilities and ensure the equipment is safe and properly maintained. Collaborate with assistant coaches, athletic trainers, and school staff to support student-athlete well-being. Represent the school positively at all meets, conferences, and related events. Qualifications: Previous competitive swimming and/or coaching experience required (high school/club or higher level required). Knowledge of swimming techniques, rules, and safety regulations. CPR, First Aid, and Lifeguard certification (or willingness to obtain prior to season). Strong organizational and communication skills. Ability to motivate student-athletes and maintain discipline and fairness. Must meet all district, state, and MHSAA (or applicable governing body) coaching certification requirements.

Job.JobTitle: Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Tennis Coach (Girls)
Job.JobDescription:
Huron Valley Schools is looking for a Tennis Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Position Title: Tennis Coach (Girls) Reports To: Building Principal/Athletic Director Location: Southfield A&T High School Start Date/End Date: March 2026-May 2026 Salary: $3,600.00 Stipend per the SEA CBA (paid at the end of the season based on the payment calendar) Position Summary: The Head Tennis Coach is responsible for managing all aspects of the high school tennis program. This includes coaching student athletes in the fundamentals and strategies of tennis, organizing practices and matches, promoting academic success and personal development, and fostering sportsmanship and team spirit. The coach is expected to create a positive, inclusive, and competitive environment that supports the school’s mission and athletic goals. Key Responsibilities: Plan and lead regular practices focused on individual skill development, team play, and physical conditioning. Instruct athletes in tennis rules, scoring, strategy, and proper techniques. Organize and supervise matches, tournaments, and team events. Ensure the safety and well-being of all student athletes during practices and competitions. Promote academic accountability and character development among team members. Coordinate match schedules, transportation, and equipment needs with the Athletic Director. Maintain inventory and oversee proper care of uniforms and tennis equipment. Communicate effectively with athletes, parents, school staff, and administration. Ensure compliance with all school, district, and state athletic policies and eligibility requirements. Encourage participation in offseason training, camps, and tennis-related activities. Serve as a role model and mentor, promoting teamwork, discipline, and respect. Qualifications: Previous coaching or competitive playing experience in tennis (high school or higher). Strong knowledge of tennis rules, techniques, and match preparation. Ability to effectively teach and motivate student-athletes of varying skill levels. Excellent communication, leadership, and organizational skills. Must meet coaching certification and clearance requirements as set by MHSAA.

Job.JobTitle: Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Wrestling Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Huron Valley Schools is looking for a Wrestling Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Job Title: Wrestling Coach Reports To: Building Principal/Athletic Director Location: Southfield A&T High School Start Date/End Date: November 2025- March 2026 Salary: $4,200.00 Stipend per the SEA CBA Job Summary: The Wrestling Coach is responsible for directing, developing, and leading the high school wrestling program in alignment with the school’s mission and athletic philosophy. The coach fosters skill development, teamwork, sportsmanship, and academic achievement while maintaining a safe and positive environment for all student-athletes. Key Responsibilities: Plan, organize, and oversee all phases of the wrestling program, including practices, matches, tournaments, and team meetings. Teach proper wrestling techniques, conditioning, and strategy while ensuring safety and compliance with rules and regulations. Develop structured practice plans that focus on technical improvement, strength, agility, and mental toughness. Promote good sportsmanship, discipline, and respect among athletes, coaches, officials, and spectators. Communicate effectively with athletes, parents, and school staff regarding schedules, expectations, and academic eligibility. Ensure compliance with all district, league, and state athletic association policies. Supervise athletes at all practices, competitions, and team functions. Maintain accurate inventory of wrestling equipment and uniforms and ensure all are in safe, usable condition. Support the academic success of student-athletes and collaborate with teachers and counselors as needed. Recruit and mentor assistant coaches to support program growth and consistency. Represent the school and program in a professional manner at all meets, clinics, and community events. Qualifications: Previous wrestling and/or wrestling coaching experience required (high school or collegiate level preferred). Strong knowledge of wrestling techniques, rules, and safety procedures. CPR, First Aid, and Concussion Awareness certification (or ability to obtain prior to the season). Excellent leadership, communication, and organizational skills. Ability to model integrity, commitment, and teamwork. Must meet all district and state athletic association certification requirements.

Job.JobTitle: Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Football Head Coach (Varsity) 2026-2027 (EXTERNAL)
Job.JobDescription:
Huron Valley Schools is looking for a Football Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Position Title: Head Football Coach Reports To: Building Principal/Athletic Director Location: Southfield A&T High School Season Dates: August 2026 - October 2026 (2026-2027 School Year - FALL ATHLETICS) Salary: $5,000.00 Stipend per the SEA CBA (paid at the end of the season based on the payment calendar) Position Summary: The Head Football Coach is responsible for overseeing all aspects of the high school football program. This includes coaching student-athletes, developing game strategies, organizing practices, ensuring academic accountability, and fostering leadership, discipline, and sportsmanship. The coach must demonstrate a strong commitment to student development and uphold the school's values both on and off the field. Key Responsibilities: Develop and implement practice plans, game strategies, and conditioning programs. Teach fundamental football techniques and advanced skills to student athletes. Promote academic success and character development among team members. Supervise assistant coaches and delegate responsibilities as appropriate. Monitor and maintain inventory of equipment and uniforms. Collaborate with the Athletic Director on scheduling games, travel, and budget needs. Ensure compliance with school, district, league, and state athletic regulations. Maintain open communication with players, parents, school staff, and the community. Foster a safe, respectful, and inclusive environment for all participants. Serve as a positive role model and mentor for student-athletes. Organize and participate in team fundraising, community outreach, and promotional events. Evaluate player performance and provide regular feedback and support for improvement. Enforce discipline and team rules consistently and fairly. Oversee offseason conditioning and training programs. Qualifications: Prior coaching experience at the high school level or higher (head coaching experience preferred). In-depth knowledge of football rules, strategies, and safety practices. Strong leadership, organizational, and communication skills. Ability to demonstrate skills and drills. Ability to motivate and mentor student athletes. Bachelor's degree in education, Sports Management, or related field preferred.

Birmingham Public Schools is seeking a Boys Volleyball Junior Varsity Coach at Groves High School for the 2025-2026 school year. Job.JobTitle: Boys Cross Country – Varsity Head Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Brandon High School is looking for a Cross Country Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION : Boys Cross Country – Varsity Head Coach – req LOCATION : Brandon High School DESCRIPTION OF THE DISTRICT : The Brandon School District is a mid-size school district nestled in Northwest Oakland County. The district has approximately 1,000 students in junior kindergarten-5 th grade between the two elementary schools, approximately 600 at the middle school level and approximately 850 at the high school. This small, tight knit community has a strong connection to its schools and is passionate about the education of its youth. Brandon High School sends 85-90% of its graduates to post-secondary school each year, it was ranked in the 82 nd percentile of all schools in the State on the top-to-bottom rankings and it was awarded as a Silver Medal Winner by the US News. Our elementary schools are both one-to-one technology are each near the 83rd percentile in State rankings. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS : High School Diploma Working with students in a similar capacity is preferred KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Must have documented evidence of organizational skills, knowledge, experience, preparation and aptitudes in the above sport commensurate with the level of the position Must have demonstrated ability to work with and motivate young people and adults Must be available during the time requirements of the position Certification in CPR/AED preferred RESPONSIBILITIES: Directly responsible for all matters relative to the program including but not limited to, player personnel, practice sessions, care and maintenance of equipment and supervision of players during practice, games, travel and other team functions. Be knowledgeable of and consistently enforce school, District, League and state association (MHSAA) rules and policies. Effectively communicate with players, officials, fellow coaches, parents, administrators and community members. Ensure that the safety and welfare of the athletes is a top priority in the organization and implementation of practice and game situations. Be responsive to instructions and directions of the district Athletic Director and other appropriate school administrators. Present a professional image and provide a positive role model in terms of personal habits, language and conduct. Responsible for press releases and public relations. Other duties as assigned. REPORTS TO : Athletic Director WORKDAY/WEEK : 20-25 hours per week in season, 10-12 hours per week out of season STARTING DATE : June 1, 2026 COMPENSATION : Per the BEA Master Agreement, Athletics Schedule C.2 POSTING DATE: November 17, 2025 POSTING DEADLINE : Internal: Until Filled External: Until Filled The Brandon Board of Education is committed to a policy of non-discrimination in relation to race, color, sex, age, religion, height, weight, gender identity, marital status, disability and national origin. For all concerns and complaints for issues relating to Title IX, Section 504, the Age Discrimination Act and Title II, please contact Megan Koslowski, Title IX Coordinator at 1025 S. Ortonville Road, Ortonville, MI 48462, (248) 627-1800.

Hazel Park Junior High (High-Needs School) is looking for a Basketball Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
2026-2027 Season 7th Grade Boys Basketball Coach Position Overview: We are seeking dedicated and enthusiastic individuals to join our team as a Junior High School Athletic Coach. As a coach, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the athletic development, character, and success of scholar-athletes. You will be responsible for coaching and mentoring scholar-athletes in various sports, fostering skill development, teamwork, and sportsmanship. This position offers an exciting opportunity to make a positive impact on the lives of young athletes while contributing to the success of our high school sports program. Responsibilities: Coach Scholar-Athletes: Provide instruction, guidance, and mentorship to student-athletes in designated sports, including but not limited to football, basketball, soccer, volleyball, baseball, softball, track and field, wrestling, swimming, tennis, and golf. Plan and Execute Practices: Develop and implement comprehensive practice plans focused on skill development, strategy, and conditioning. Create a positive and engaging practice environment that fosters learning and growth. Strategic Game Planning: Develop game strategies, tactics, and playbooks to maximize team performance during competitions. Make real-time adjustments and decisions during games to optimize team success. Player Development: Assess individual player strengths and weaknesses and provide personalized coaching to enhance skill development and performance. Encourage continuous improvement and set realistic goals for player development. Team Building and Leadership: Foster a positive team culture based on sportsmanship, respect, and teamwork. Promote leadership skills, communication, and collaboration among team members. Academic Emphasis: Instill the importance of academic excellence and balance between athletics and academics among scholar-athletes. Encourage and support scholar-athletes in achieving academic success by monitoring their progress, providing academic resources, and promoting time management and study skills. Collaborate with teachers and administrators to ensure scholar-athletes are meeting academic requirements and maintaining eligibility for participation in sports. Foster a culture of academic achievement and personal growth that prepares scholar-athletes for success both on and off the field. Communication: Maintain open and transparent communication with parents/guardians, school administration, the community and the Athletic department regarding team activities, expectations, and schedules. Build positive relationships with stakeholders to support the success of the sports program. Compliance and Safety: Supervise players during practices, games, travel and other team functions. Ensure compliance with all applicable rules, regulations, and guidelines set forth by the Michigan High School Athletic Association (MHSAA) and the school district. Prioritize the safety and well-being of student-athletes at all times. Be responsive to direction given by the Athletic Director and other appropriate school administrators. Professional Development: Stay informed about current coaching techniques, trends, and best practices in high school sports coaching. Attend coaching clinics, workshops, and training sessions to enhance coaching knowledge and skills. Equipment Management: Assist in tracking and caring for equipment and facilities related to the sport. Qualifications: Previous coaching experience at the high school level or higher preferred. Demonstrated expertise in the designated sport(s), including knowledge of rules, techniques, and strategies. Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills. Ability to motivate, inspire, and mentor scholar-athletes to reach their full potential. Commitment to fostering a positive team culture based on integrity, sportsmanship, and respect. Availability to attend practices, games, and team events during evenings and weekends. Valid Concussion Certification or willingness to obtain certification as required by the MHSAA. Must adhere to the MHSAA policies for coaches. CPR/AED & First Aid Certification required. Must submit to a Finger-Print based Criminal History Record Information (CHRI) check in accordance with the Michigan School Volunteer & Employee Criminal History Program. Note: This job description is intended to outline the general nature and level of work performed by individuals assigned to this position. It is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and qualifications required of coaches in Michigan high schools. Application Procedure: All Non-Hazel Park Schools Employees will be required to complete an application through EduStaff and will be compensated as a contract employee of EduStaff. Application Deadline: When Filled. All interested applicants need to complete the employment application. Applications will be accepted until the position is filled. The Hazel Park School District does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, age, disability, height, weight, religion, or marital status in its programs and activities. WE ARE AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

Encourage and support scholar-athletes in achieving academic success by monitoring their progress, providing academic resources, and promoting time management and study skills. Collaborate with teachers and administrators to ensure scholar-athletes are meeting academic requirements and maintaining eligibility for participation in sports. Foster a culture of academic achievement and personal growth that prepares scholar-athletes for success both on and off the field. Communication: Maintain open and transparent communication with parents/guardians, school administration, the community and the Athletic department regarding team activities, expectations, and schedules. Build positive relationships with stakeholders to support the success of the sports program. Compliance and Safety: Supervise players during practices, games, travel and other team functions. Ensure compliance with all applicable rules, regulations, and guidelines set forth by the Michigan High School Athletic Association (MHSAA) and the school district. Prioritize the safety and well-being of student-athletes at all times. Be responsive to direction given by the Athletic Director and other appropriate school administrators. Professional Development: Stay informed about current coaching techniques, trends, and best practices in high school sports coaching. Attend coaching clinics, workshops, and training sessions to enhance coaching knowledge and skills. Equipment Management: Assist in tracking and caring for equipment and facilities related to the sport. Qualifications: Previous coaching experience at the high school level or higher preferred. Demonstrated expertise in the designated sport(s), including knowledge of rules, techniques, and strategies. Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills. Ability to motivate, inspire, and mentor scholar-athletes to reach their full potential. Commitment to fostering a positive team culture based on integrity, sportsmanship, and respect. Availability to attend practices, games, and team events during evenings and weekends. Valid Concussion Certification or willingness to obtain certification as required by the MHSAA. Must adhere to the MHSAA policies for coaches. CPR/AED & First Aid Certification required. Must submit to a Finger-Print based Criminal History Record Information (CHRI) check in accordance with the Michigan School Volunteer & Employee Criminal History Program. Note: This job description is intended to outline the general nature and level of work performed by individuals assigned to this position. It is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and qualifications required of coaches in Michigan high schools. As a coach, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the athletic development, character, and success of scholar-athletes. You will be responsible for coaching and mentoring scholar-athletes in various sports, fostering skill development, teamwork, and sportsmanship. This position offers an exciting opportunity to make a positive impact on the lives of young athletes while contributing to the success of our high school sports program. Responsibilities: Coach Scholar-Athletes: Provide instruction, guidance, and mentorship to student-athletes in designated sports, including but not limited to football, basketball, soccer, volleyball, baseball, softball, track and field, wrestling, swimming, tennis, and golf. Plan and Execute Practices: Develop and implement comprehensive practice plans focused on skill development, strategy, and conditioning. Create a positive and engaging practice environment that fosters learning and growth. Strategic Game Planning: Develop game strategies, tactics, and playbooks to maximize team performance during competitions. Make real-time adjustments and decisions during games to optimize team success. Player Development: Assess individual player strengths and weaknesses and provide personalized coaching to enhance skill development and performance. Encourage continuous improvement and set realistic goals for player development. Team Building and Leadership: Foster a positive team culture based on sportsmanship, respect, and teamwork. Promote leadership skills, communication, and collaboration among team members. Academic Emphasis: Instill the importance of academic excellence and balance between athletics and academics among scholar-athletes. Encourage and support scholar-athletes in achieving academic success by monitoring their progress, providing academic resources, and promoting time management and study skills. Collaborate with teachers and administrators to ensure scholar-athletes are meeting academic requirements and maintaining eligibility for participation in sports. Foster a culture of academic achievement and personal growth that prepares scholar-athletes for success both on and off the field. Communication: Maintain open and transparent communication with parents/guardians, school administration, the community and the Athletic department regarding team activities, expectations, and schedules. Build positive relationships with stakeholders to support the success of the sports program. Compliance and Safety: Supervise players during practices, games, travel and other team functions. Ensure compliance with all applicable rules, regulations, and guidelines set forth by the Michigan High School Athletic Association (MHSAA) and the school district. Prioritize the safety and well-being of student-athletes at all times. Be responsive to direction given by the Athletic Director and other appropriate school administrators. Professional Development: Stay informed about current coaching techniques, trends, and best practices in high school sports coaching. Attend coaching clinics, workshops, and training sessions to enhance coaching knowledge and skills. Equipment Management: Assist in tracking and caring for equipment and facilities related to the sport. Qualifications: Previous coaching experience at the high school level or higher preferred. Demonstrated expertise in the designated sport(s), including knowledge of rules, techniques, and strategies. Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills. Ability to motivate, inspire, and mentor scholar-athletes to reach their full potential. Commitment to fostering a positive team culture based on integrity, sportsmanship, and respect. Availability to attend practices, games, and team events during evenings and weekends. Valid Concussion Certification or willingness to obtain certification as required by the MHSAA. Must adhere to the MHSAA policies for coaches. CPR/AED & First Aid Certification required. Must submit to a Finger-Print based Criminal History Record Information (CHRI) check in accordance with the Michigan School Volunteer & Employee Criminal History Program. Note: This job description is intended to outline the general nature and level of work performed by individuals assigned to this position. It is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and qualifications required of coaches in Michigan high schools. Application Procedure: All Non-Hazel Park Schools Employees will be required to complete an application through EduStaff and will be compensated as a contract employee of EduStaff. Application Deadline: When Filled. All interested applicants need to complete the employment application. Applications will be accepted until the position is filled. The Hazel Park School District does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, age, disability, height, weight, religion, or marital status in its programs and activities. WE ARE AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

Must adhere to the MHSAA policies for coaches. CPR/AED & First Aid Certification required. Must submit to a Finger-Print based Criminal History Record Information (CHRI) check in accordance with the Michigan School Volunteer & Employee Criminal History Program. Note: This job description is intended to outline the general nature and level of work performed by individuals assigned to this position. It is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and qualifications required of coaches in Michigan high schools. Hazel Park High School (High-Needs School) is looking for a Basketball Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
2026-2027 Season Junior Varsity Boys Basketball Coach Position Overview: We are seeking dedicated and enthusiastic individuals to join our team as a High School Athletic Coach. As a coach, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the athletic development, character, and success of scholar-athletes. You will be responsible for coaching and mentoring scholar-athletes in various sports, fostering skill development, teamwork, and sportsmanship. This position offers an exciting opportunity to make a positive impact on the lives of young athletes while contributing to the success of our high school sports program. Responsibilities: Coach Scholar-Athletes: Provide instruction, guidance, and mentorship to student-athletes in designated sports, including but not limited to football, basketball, soccer, volleyball, baseball, softball, track and field, wrestling, swimming, tennis, and golf. Plan and Execute Practices: Develop and implement comprehensive practice plans focused on skill development, strategy, and conditioning. Create a positive and engaging practice environment that fosters learning and growth. Strategic Game Planning: Develop game strategies, tactics, and playbooks to maximize team performance during competitions. Make real-time adjustments and decisions during games to optimize team success. Player Development: Assess individual player strengths and weaknesses and provide personalized coaching to enhance skill development and performance. Encourage continuous improvement and set realistic goals for player development. Team Building and Leadership: Foster a positive team culture based on sportsmanship, respect, and teamwork. Promote leadership skills, communication, and collaboration among team members. Academic Emphasis: Instill the importance of academic excellence and balance between athletics and academics among scholar-athletes. Encourage and support scholar-athletes in achieving academic success by monitoring their progress, providing academic resources, and promoting time management and study skills. Collaborate with teachers and administrators to ensure scholar-athletes are meeting academic requirements and maintaining eligibility for participation in sports. Foster a culture of academic achievement and personal growth that prepares scholar-athletes for success both on and off the field. Communication: Maintain open and transparent communication with parents/guardians, school administration, the community and the Athletic department regarding team activities, expectations, and schedules. Build positive relationships with stakeholders to support the success of the sports program. Compliance and Safety: Supervise players during practices, games, travel and other team functions. Ensure compliance with all applicable rules, regulations, and guidelines set forth by the Michigan High School Athletic Association (MHSAA) and the school district. Prioritize the safety and well-being of student-athletes at all times. Be responsive to direction given by the Athletic Director and other appropriate school administrators. Professional Development: Stay informed about current coaching techniques, trends, and best practices in high school sports coaching. Attend coaching clinics, workshops, and training sessions to enhance coaching knowledge and skills. Equipment Management: Assist in tracking and caring for equipment and facilities related to the sport. Qualifications: Previous coaching experience at the high school level or higher preferred. Demonstrated expertise in the designated sport(s), including knowledge of rules, techniques, and strategies. Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills. Ability to motivate, inspire, and mentor scholar-athletes to reach their full potential. Commitment to fostering a positive team culture based on integrity, sportsmanship, and respect. Availability to attend practices, games, and team events during evenings and weekends. Valid Concussion Certification or willingness to obtain certification as required by the MHSAA. Must adhere to the MHSAA policies for coaches. CPR/AED & First Aid Certification required. Must submit to a Finger-Print based Criminal History Record Information (CHRI) check in accordance with the Michigan School Volunteer & Employee Criminal History Program. Note: This job description is intended to outline the general nature and level of work performed by individuals assigned to this position. It is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and qualifications required of coaches in Michigan high schools. Application Procedure: All Non-Hazel Park Schools Employees will be required to complete an application through EduStaff and will be compensated as a contract employee of EduStaff. Application Deadline: When Filled. All interested applicants need to complete the employment application. Applications will be accepted until the position is filled. The Hazel Park School District does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, age, disability, height, weight, religion, or marital status in its programs and activities. WE ARE AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

Job.JobTitle: Head Boys Track and Field Coach - Muir Middle School
Job.JobDescription:
Muir Middle School is looking for a Track & Field Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION DESCRIPTION Huron Valley Schools is seeking an enthusiastic, energetic, and innovative Head Boys' Track & Field Coach. Position Type: Athletics/Activities/Coaching Location : Muir Middle School Salary: Per contract Schedule B Reports To : Athletic Director Status : School Year 2025 - 2026 Start Date: ASAP MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS Able to instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy, and physical training necessary for athletes to realize a degree of individual and team success Coaching experience preferred Playing experience at the high school or college level is preferred ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS Provide proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites Perform duties which may be assigned by the Athletic Director Instills in each player a respect for equipment, and school property The position includes other unusual aspects such as extended time, risk injury factors and due process predicaments Implement and design clinics in order to promote the sport Schedule all practices, clinics, camps and competitions Continue to grow professionally by attending seminars and clinics Maintains discipline and works to increase morale and cooperation EDUCATION and/or EXPERIENCE: High School diploma and/or related experience. LANGUAGE, MATHEMATICAL AND REASONING SKILLS: Ability to speak and write effectively. Ability to apply mathematical concepts to practical situations. Ability to carry out instructions furnished in written or oral format and deal with problems involving several concrete variables in standardized situations. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to sit, talk, or hear. The employee is occasionally required to stand, walk and reach with hands or arms. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision and ability to adjust and focus. WORK ENVIRONMENT: The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. METHOD OF APPLICATION A cover letter is required in order to be considered for this position and should be attached as the first page of your resume. The cover letter should outline skills and experience that directly relate to the qualifications for the position. Please send letter of intent and coaching resume to: Jim Marszalek Milford High School Athletic Director jim.marszalek@hvs.org Inquiries should be addressed to: Jeanna Klebba Human Resources Administrative Assistant 2390 South Milford Rd., Highland, MI 48357 248.684.8217 (TELEPHONE) • 248.684.8266 (FAX) E-MAIL: jeanna.klebba@hvs.org Jeanette Wenger, Chief Human Resource Officer and TitleVI Officer
